scope: Release reflection for the committed Abyssal Surge static client, its Pages artifact, compressed cinematic, and browser-facing campaign controls.

evidence.subject: Focused state, mirror, narration-atlas, and release-closure regression coverage.
evidence.observation: The selected release tests passed 34 of 34: deterministic campaign progression, mirror validation/fallback, atlas provenance/runtime contracts, Pages closure, reward assets, TWA base URL, and narration synchronization.

evidence.subject: Shipped opt-in cinematic validates as browser-compatible compressed media.
evidence.observation: The 2,363,629-byte cinematic is H.264 High/yuv420p at 960x540 and 30 fps, has faststart metadata, and decoded successfully.

evidence.subject: Running-browser campaign control smoke test on the local release candidate.
evidence.observation: Playwriter snapshots showed the branded Cinder Span view, Korean-to-English language transition, retry persistence status, Boss Specification panel, and successful transition into the Ember Break combat view. Browser logs were empty after each successful interaction.
evidence.command: Playwriter observe -> language toggle -> retry -> boss specification -> start combat -> observe

evidence.subject: GitHub Pages workflow built and deployed the committed release artifact.
evidence.observation: GitHub Actions run 29543885796 completed successfully for commit 62ab111 on main after the repository was renamed to jellyggumi/Abyssal-Surge.

evidence.subject: Branded public Pages URL serves the Abyssal Surge campaign document.
evidence.observation: A public HTTP read returned the deployed campaign content, including the offline-ready status, three-stage campaign copy, optional cinematic notice, and local-save text; the previous 404 path was resolved by repository rename.

next_stage_decision.rationale: The release evidence validates the selected local behavior, Pages artifact, public URL, and compressed cinematic. The remaining decision is human review of presentation, narration tone, and provider-generated art quality rather than an unverified release defect.
